data.title: China moves to stop big local brokers from stoking domestic interest in stablecoins
data.description:
Chinese regulators have instructed major domestic brokerages to halt the publication of research and public commentary related to stablecoins. The move comes as China sees a rising wave of domestic interest in stable digital assets, which is reportedly causing concern among mainland authorities who remain opposed to most cryptocurrency activity
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/china-stop-local-brokers-from-stablecoins/

data.title: Analyst Outlines How Production Cost Determines XRP Price, But Is It Better Than Bitcoin?
data.description:
A fresh debate in the crypto space has emerged over whether the cost of production significantly impacts the XRP price and the value of Bitcoin (BTC). Market expert CrediBULL Crypto has outlined how these costs influence XRP’s value compared to Bitcoin, concluding that both cryptocurrencies follow the same pricing formula. XRP Price Formula Mirrors That Of Bitcoin A recent discourse on X social media has reignited discussions on whether production costs play a decisive role in determining the prices of cryptocurrencies. CrediBULL Crypto weighed in, explaining that both Bitcoin and XRP follow the same fundamental pricing model, where the cost to produce, combined with speculative and utility value, determines the market price
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/xrp/production-cost-xrp-price-2/

data.title: Bolivians turn to crypto en masse as inflation soars and dollars dry up
data.description:

data.title: BlackRock Confirms NO Plan for Solana or XRP ETF: Is This a Bullish or Bearish Sign?
data.description:
BlackRock has officially stated that there are currently no plans to launch an exchange-traded fund (ETF) focused on either Solana or XRP, prompting a significant discussion in the investment community. This announcement raises important questions about the potential implications for the future performance of these cryptocurrencies. Some investors are left wondering whether the absence of an ETF signifies a bearish trend, indicating a lack of institutional interest and support for Solana and XRP, while others believe it could be a bullish sign, suggesting that investors may have an opportunity to buy in at lower prices before any significant developments take place. As the market reacts to this news, many are analyzing the potential consequences for both short-term trading and long-term investment strategies in the ever-evolving landscape of cryptocurrency.
source: https://blocknews.com/blackrock-confirms-no-plan-for-solana-or-xrp-etf-is-this-a-bullish-or-bearish-sign/

data.title: Trump’s nominee for CFTC lead Brian Quintenz in limbo as backers withdraw support

Brian Quintenz once served on the CFTC as commissioner and is an executive at the crypto arm of […]

data.title: Chainlink Reveals Strategic LINK Reserve: What Does This Mean for LINK Holders?
data.description:

This development raises important questions regarding its implications for LINK holders and the potential impact on the overall market dynamics of this digital asset.
